answer:
The Fanbasis API requires authentication via an x-api-key header [1]. You must include your unique API key in every request to the server [1][2]. While some implementations may pass the key via an Authorization bearer token [3], the primary documented method is the x-api-key header [1]. There is no support for OAuth or client ID and secret pairs [2].

Example using cURL:
```bash
curl -X GET https://www.fanbasis.com/public-api/ \
  -H "x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY"
```

For server-side TypeScript or JavaScript, you can use the official Checkout SDK by initializing the client with your key [4]:
```js
const client = new FanbasisCheckout({
  apiKey: process.env['FANBASIS_CHECKOUT_API_KEY']
});
```
